title: derris rubrocalyx subsp. acuminata
intro: Derris rubrocalyx subsp. acuminata is a plant species in the legume family, known for its toxic and medicinal properties. It is primarily used to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Derris rubrocalyx subsp. acuminata typically involve drying and processing the leaves or roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or topical ointments for medicinal use.
